summary: Martin Fowler briefly notes the continued absence of a comprehensive book on UML activity diagrams, a gap he had previously identified in his book UML Distilled. He recommends Conrad Bock's articles on UML 2.0 as a valuable resource that covers activity diagrams in greater depth. Fowler adds credibility to this recommendation by noting that Bock was one of the leaders of the activity diagram work in UML 2.
key_points: There remains no good comprehensive book specifically teaching UML activity diagrams
Conrad Bock's UML 2.0 articles provide deeper coverage of activity diagrams than UML Distilled
Conrad Bock has authoritative expertise as a leader of the UML 2 activity diagram specification work
money_quotes: I bemoaned the fact that there isn't a good book on teaching UML's activity diagrams. There still isn't.
key_insight: For in-depth understanding of UML activity diagrams, practitioners should turn to Conrad Bock's articles rather than waiting for a comprehensive book that may never materialize.
